_notes: Functional test vectors for the step(state, new_direction) -> state function defined in spec-step.md.
Each case's `input` is a full world-model state plus a `new_direction` field: one of "UP"/"DOWN"/"LEFT"/"RIGHT", or null if no new direction arrived since the last step. `expect` is either:
{ "result": "assertion_error" }  (input fails validation, per the Step Function's Validation step)

Groups mirror the Behavior subsections of the spec: a successful move, the three ways a move can kill the snake, validation failures, new_direction-driven direction changes, and the already-dead no-op case.
step() never revives a dead snake: if the input state has alive=false, step() returns it unchanged and ignores new_direction entirely (see group `already_dead`).

groups.name: successful_move
groups.description: Candidate tile is open: snake advances, grows, and scores.

cases.name: simple_move_grows_and_scores
cases.description: Open square ahead: snake advances, leaves a body tile behind, score increments.

groups.name: death_by_collision
groups.description: Candidate tile is occupied by a WALL or SNAKE_BODY: snake dies in place, tiles unchanged.

cases.name: move_into_wall_kills_snake
cases.description: Candidate tile is a WALL: snake dies in place, tiles unchanged.

cases.name: move_into_own_body_kills_snake
cases.description: Candidate tile is a SNAKE_BODY tile: snake dies in place, tiles unchanged.

groups.name: death_by_bounds
groups.description: Candidate tile falls outside the grid: snake dies in place, tiles unchanged.

cases.name: move_out_of_bounds_top_edge_kills_snake
cases.description: Snake at y=0 moving UP would leave the grid: dies in place.

cases.name: move_out_of_bounds_right_edge_kills_snake
cases.description: Snake at max x moving RIGHT would leave the grid: dies in place.

groups.name: validation_failures
groups.description: Input states that violate the Validation step's invariants: step() raises rather than producing a next state.

cases.name: validation_rejects_tile_out_of_bounds
cases.description: A grid_tiles entry outside [0, width) x [0, height) fails validation.

cases.name: validation_rejects_snake_pos_out_of_bounds
cases.description: snake_pos outside grid bounds fails validation.

cases.name: validation_rejects_alive_snake_overlapping_tile
cases.description: alive=true but snake_pos coincides with an existing tile fails validation.

cases.name: validation_rejects_invalid_new_direction
cases.description: A non-null new_direction that isn't one of the four directions fails validation.

groups.name: new_direction_direction_changes
groups.description: A non-null new_direction overrides snake_next for this step before the move is computed; a null new_direction leaves it untouched.

cases.name: new_direction_overrides_snake_next_before_move
cases.description: snake_next is UP but new_direction is RIGHT: the move uses RIGHT, and the returned state's snake_next is RIGHT.

cases.name: new_direction_into_wall_kills_snake
cases.description: new_direction redirects the snake straight into a wall tile it wasn't already heading toward.

cases.name: new_direction_reversal_into_neck_kills_snake
cases.description: new_direction is the exact opposite of snake_next: applied as-is (not rejected), so the candidate move lands on the snake's own trailing body tile and it dies.

groups.name: already_dead
groups.description: Input snake is already dead (alive=false): step() is a no-op regardless of what the candidate move would have done, and regardless of new_direction.

cases.name: dead_snake_state_is_unchanged
cases.description: alive=false with snake_pos coinciding with a tile passes validation (the overlap check only applies when alive=true). step() never revives a dead snake, so the output equals the input exactly, with no movement, growth, scoring, or new_direction evaluated (note the new_direction is ignored even though it differs from snake_next).
